complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Database Schema and TypeORM Migrations
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the implementation of database schema and TypeORM migrations for UIFlow and UIComponent entities into specific subtasks, including entity definition, relationship mapping, migration creation, testing, and documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves database modeling, TypeORM configuration, and migration setup. It requires understanding of entity relationships and database design principles. The complexity comes from ensuring proper relationships between entities and following existing patterns.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create IUINode Interface and UINodeBase Class
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the implementation of IUINode interface and UINodeBase class into specific subtasks, including interface definition, base class implementation, integration with existing systems, unit testing, and documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task requires extending an existing architecture with new interfaces and base classes. It involves understanding the current INode system and ensuring compatibility. Integration with logging, error handling, and monitoring systems adds complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the extension of NodesPool for UI components into specific subtasks, including factory method creation, registration mechanisms, component categorization, backward compatibility testing, and documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Modifying an existing system while ensuring backward compatibility is complex. This task requires deep understanding of the current NodesPool architecture and plugin patterns. The categorization system adds another layer of complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the implementation of the basic UI component library into specific subtasks for each component category (Container, Form, Display, Action), including component design, implementation, integration with Material-UI, testing, and documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves creating multiple component types with different behaviors and integrations. Each component category has unique requirements (persistence, caching, queue integration). Using Material-UI and ensuring all components follow the interface adds complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the ReactFlow canvas integration into specific subtasks, including canvas extension, drag-and-drop implementation, preview system, multi-view support, UI consistency, and testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Integrating ReactFlow with an existing canvas system is complex. The real-time preview and multi-view support add significant complexity. Ensuring consistent look and feel with the existing builder requires careful UI work.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create Express.js API Endpoints for FastUI
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the creation of Express.js API endpoints into specific subtasks, including controller implementation for CRUD operations, endpoint design for templates/deployment/preview, validation/error handling, authentication integration, and API documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves extending an existing API structure with new endpoints. It requires understanding of the current Express.js setup and middleware. The complexity comes from ensuring proper validation, error handling, and authentication.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the implementation of the component property editor into specific subtasks, including property panel extension, form control implementation, style system integration, real-time preview functionality, and UI consistency testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Extending the property panel for UI components involves complex UI work. The real-time preview and dynamic property updates add complexity. The style system integration with the platform theme requires careful design.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Event System for UI Components
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the implementation of the event system into specific subtasks, including Observer pattern extension, QueueManager integration, async operations with BullMQ, state management integration, logging/monitoring integration, and testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Event systems are inherently complex, especially when integrated with queue management and async operations. The integration with multiple existing systems (QueueManager, BullMQ, state management, logging) adds significant complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the agent integration system implementation into specific subtasks, including flow execution integration, parameter detection via DataSource, WebSocket preview setup, variable mapping with credential management, CachePool integration, and testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves complex integration between UI components and agent flows. It touches multiple systems (flow execution, WebSockets, credential management, caching) and requires real-time functionality. The automatic parameter detection adds significant complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the template system implementation into specific subtasks, including template creation/management, branding support, versioning system, template selection UI, and import/export functionality.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Creating a template system involves complex data structures and versioning. The branding elements and default behaviors add complexity. The import/export functionality requires careful serialization and validation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the deployment system implementation into specific subtasks, including Docker pipeline integration, embeddable interface creation, Git workflow setup, API key authentication, CORS/rate limiting configuration, and testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Deployment systems are complex by nature. Integration with Docker, Git, and authentication systems adds significant complexity. The embeddable interface and security considerations (CORS, rate limiting) require careful implementation.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Advanced Component System with Caching
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the advanced component system implementation into specific subtasks, including conditional rendering, dynamic content support, CachePool integration, performance optimization, and lazy loading implementation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Advanced component features like conditional rendering and dynamic content are complex. The caching integration and performance optimization require deep understanding of rendering cycles. Lazy loading adds another layer of complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Multi-View System with State Management
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the multi-view system implementation into specific subtasks, including view management architecture, state management across views, navigation implementation, state persistence/restoration, consistent rendering, and testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Multi-view systems with shared state are inherently complex. Managing state across views, navigation, and persistence requires sophisticated architecture. Ensuring consistent rendering and behavior across views adds significant complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the analytics integration into specific subtasks, including tracking implementation, monitoring system integration, dashboard creation, error tracking setup, and usage statistics collection.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Analytics integration involves multiple tracking points and integration with existing systems. The dashboard creation and usage statistics require data processing and visualization. The complexity is moderate compared to other tasks.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the advanced Kubernetes deployment implementation into specific subtasks, including Kubernetes configuration, scaling/high availability setup, networking/security configuration, environment templates, monitoring integration, logging setup, and documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Kubernetes deployment is highly complex, involving container orchestration, networking, security, and scaling. The high availability requirements and environment-specific configurations add significant complexity. Monitoring and logging integration for deployed instances requires additional work.
